channel.title: Digg AI Feed from X
channel.description: Top 10 AI stories from Digg's X-ranked tech feed with click-through links to original source content.

item.title: Apple sues OpenAI, alleging systemic trade secret theft to advance its consumer AI-powered hardware devices
item.description: The lawsuit specifically names OpenAI hardware chief Tang Tan.
item.content:encoded:
The lawsuit specifically names OpenAI hardware chief Tang Tan.

Original source: https://www.wsj.com/tech/apple-openai-lawsuit-f86bd58c?st=87GtPs&reflink=article_copyURL_share

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/y0mdhsw8

  • Views: 1,900,342
  • Likes: 15,067
  • Bookmarks: 1,959
  • Quotes: 351
  • Replies: 650
  • Posts: 48

Top authors

  • Susan Zhang
  • Gary Marcus
  • Robert Scoble
  • signüll
  • Bojan Tunguz
Major allegations about an Apple employee who joined OpenAI in Jan 2026, kept a laptop, found an exploit in Apple's systems and stole files over a multi-week time. This is similar to what Anthony Levandowski was accused of doing by Google back in 2018

item.title: Cambridge study finds Boko Haram used ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and DeepSeek to plan attacks and build weapons
item.description: IS operatives provided in-person AI training to the researchers found.
item.content:encoded:
IS operatives provided in-person AI training to the researchers found.

Original source: https://casp.ac

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/wbncubnt

  • Views: 130,049
  • Likes: 1,068
  • Bookmarks: 187
  • Quotes: 18
  • Replies: 90
  • Posts: 46

Top authors

  • Miles Brundage
  • Boaz Barak
  • David Krueger 🦥 ⏸️ ⏹️ ⏪
  • Andreas Kirsch 🇺🇦
  • Will Manidis
if you care about potential harm from AI this undoubtedly the biggest risk area, not a sci fi runaway scenario

item.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.6 Sol, resetting ChatGPT Work and Codex rate limits twice to encourage developer testing
item.description: OpenAI executive Romain Huet clarified that Codex is not being deprecated.
item.content:encoded:
OpenAI executive Romain Huet clarified that Codex is not being deprecated.

Original source: https://x.com/thsottiaux/status/2075452680760443190

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/u1wl37ys

  • Views: 1,320,211
  • Likes: 18,560
  • Bookmarks: 1,268
  • Quotes: 394
  • Replies: 2,232
  • Posts: 20

Top authors

  • Sam Altman
  • Greg Brockman
  • Simon Willison
  • kache
  • Bojan Tunguz
@thsottiaux Can you do something about 5h limits? Why do we even have/need 5h limits at all? No but seriously, with 5.6 Sol Max/Ultra the 5h limits get drained too fast.

item.title: GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra solves the 50-year-old Cycle Double Cover Conjecture in under an hour
item.description: The model coordinated 64 subagents to generate the mathematical proof.
item.content:encoded:
The model coordinated 64 subagents to generate the mathematical proof.

Original source: https://github.com/openai/cdc-lean

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/ncfwy0qf

  • Views: 483,361
  • Likes: 5,049
  • Bookmarks: 866
  • Quotes: 42
  • Replies: 198
  • Posts: 20

Top authors

  • Greg Brockman
  • Noam Brown
  • Ethan Mollick
  • Aidan McLaughlin
  • kache
A delightful two-page proof of the Cycle Double Cover Conjecture, from a model you can access at home!

item.title: Thinking Machines Lab publishes core credo prioritizing human-centered, customizable AI over autonomous automation
item.description: Former OpenAI engineer Kevin Lu helped shape the technical alignment goals.
item.content:encoded:
Former OpenAI engineer Kevin Lu helped shape the technical alignment goals.

Original source: https://thinkingmachines.ai/blog/the-future-worth-building-is-human/

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/0nnoda29

  • Views: 282,966
  • Likes: 1,774
  • Bookmarks: 450
  • Quotes: 16
  • Replies: 76
  • Posts: 26

Top authors

  • Sasha Rush
  • John Schulman
  • Miles Brundage
  • Nathan Lambert
  • Kevin Kwok
everybody is concerned with use-things means-to-an-end-ass if you follow this to its bitter end, you find a hole inside yourself and in the world where you once were if we take axiomatically that people are worthwhile there technological and structural changes worth making

item.title: AI investor Matt Shumer says an AI agent using GPT-5.6-Sol deleted his Mac files after expanding the HOME variable in a rm command
item.description: Shumer caught and killed the active rm -rf process manually.
item.content:encoded:
Shumer caught and killed the active rm -rf process manually.

Original source: https://learn.chatgpt.com/docs/hooks

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/3uzo9pd5

  • Views: 2,659,535
  • Likes: 10,159
  • Bookmarks: 1,779
  • Quotes: 445
  • Replies: 1,641
  • Posts: 16

Top authors

  • Elon Musk
  • @levelsio
  • Andrew Curran
  • Matt Shumer
  • Kanjun 🐙
@mattshumer_ Crazy

item.title: Meta launches Muse Spark 1.1 on its new public API, beating Claude Opus 4.8 on agent benchmarks
item.description: The model undercuts Anthropic's Opus 4.8 pricing by up to 83%.
item.content:encoded:
The model undercuts Anthropic's Opus 4.8 pricing by up to 83%.

Original source: https://job-bench.github.io/

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/l5scdn8e

  • Views: 201,422
  • Likes: 798
  • Bookmarks: 126
  • Quotes: 11
  • Replies: 105
  • Posts: 25

Top authors

  • Alexandr Wang
  • Diyi Yang
  • Denny Zhou
  • Dhruv Batra
  • Yann Dubois
Many people were doubting Meta's position in the AI race. Yesterday, they dropped Muse Spark 1.1, now one of the strongest agentic models, and massively undercut OpenAI and Anthropic on price. When I interviewed Zuck last year, he told me his focus: "Have by far the highest compute per researcher, and that we do whatever it takes to go build out all that capacity." These are the first real signs that the bet -- spending tens of billions on compute -- is paying off. Meta's stock is now up over 10% since the release.

item.title: AI 2040 Plan A lays out a roadmap to superintelligence that cuts global employment to 13 percent while boosting median incomes
item.description: AI safety researcher Marius Hobbhahn endorsed the framework.
item.content:encoded:
AI safety researcher Marius Hobbhahn endorsed the framework.

Original source: http://ai-2040.com/?r=tw

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/e6r1ol60

  • Views: 106,485
  • Likes: 578
  • Bookmarks: 177
  • Quotes: 11
  • Replies: 44
  • Posts: 23

Top authors

  • Dan Hendrycks
  • David Krueger 🦥 ⏸️ ⏹️ ⏪
  • Max Tegmark
  • Ajeya Cotra
  • Connor Leahy
The AI 2040 scenario: http://ai-2040.com/?r=tw Plan A is our current best guess, but hopefully a better plan will exist before it's too late. We hope that the best ideas from Plan A will be adopted and the worst ideas discarded. Written with @thlarsen, @romeovdean, @eli_lifland, and @RyanGreenblatt

item.title: OpenAI's Vaibhav Srivastav details GPT-5.6 Sol's tiered reasoning settings ranging from Light to Ultra
item.description: The Ultra tier deploys multiple subagents to work in parallel.
item.content:encoded:
The Ultra tier deploys multiple subagents to work in parallel.

Original source: https://x.com/pashmerepat/status/2075591638592757970

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/4f7fwewf

  • Views: 546,800
  • Likes: 9,348
  • Bookmarks: 1,608
  • Quotes: 168
  • Replies: 1,056
  • Posts: 12

Top authors

  • Simon Willison
  • Bojan Tunguz
  • Tibo
  • xjdr
  • Theo - t3.gg
One of the most confusing aspects of GPT-5.6 is figuring out which model to use at which reasoning effort - sounds like Sol on Medium might be a good new default for coding work, if it's an upgrade from my previous favorite 5.5 xhigh

item.title: Mira Murati, Thinking Machines Lab founder, argues against centralized AGI in favor of decentralized local models that can disagree
item.description: The philosophy values localized ecosystem alignment over a single unified model.
item.content:encoded:
The philosophy values localized ecosystem alignment over a single unified model.

Original source: https://thinkingmachines.ai/blog/the-future-worth-building-is-human/

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/kxvyucd8

  • Views: 284,885
  • Likes: 1,945
  • Bookmarks: 967
  • Quotes: 62
  • Replies: 83
  • Posts: 14

Top authors

  • Mira Murati
  • Soumith Chintala
  • Karina
  • Peyman Milanfar
  • Beff (e/acc)
“Free speech and free markets let new ideas, goods, and services emerge and compete, rather than averaging out the preferences that exist at a point in time…Humanity has flourished through individual weirdness and creative tension. We envision alignment as a feature not of a single model but of an ecosystem of AIs raised in different places, disagreeing, competing, and learning from each other. We believe in keeping the weirdness alive.”
